meanings.usage: 指任何能够伤害或杀死生物的物质

meanings.meaning: 不合逻辑的；没有道理的
meanings.usage: 形容人的行为或想法缺乏逻辑性
指特别美味或珍贵的食物

meanings.meaning: 令人厌恶的；令人反感的
meanings.usage: 描述让人觉得恶心或排斥的事物
一种海洋生物，常被当作食物
表达对某人或某事的强烈不喜欢或蔑视
形容特别珍贵或被珍视的东西，如prize plants获奖植物
指突然产生的强烈欲望或想法，如on impulse一时冲动
指因意外或失望而产生的震惊和沮丧感
指拥有或控制某物，take possession of表示接管、占领
指昂贵的享受或物品，a great luxury表示非常奢侈的享受
指把不同事物联系在一起，associate A with B把A和B联系起来
指从某处逃出来，或避免受到伤害
描述做某事时内心不愿意但又不得不做的状态

phrases.phrase: One man's meat is another man's poison
phrases.usage: 谚语，意思是甲之蜜糖，乙之砒霜。指不同的人对同一种事物有不同的喜好或评价

examples.en: One man's meat is another man's poison - some people love coffee but others find it terrible.
examples.zh: 萝卜青菜各有所爱——有人爱咖啡，有人却觉得它很糟糕。

phrases.usage: 坚持；坚守；维持原状。指坚持做某事或坚持某种习惯

examples.en: We stick to our original plan despite the difficulties.
examples.zh: 尽管有困难，我们仍然坚持原计划。

examples.en: Most people stick to the foods they grew up with.
examples.zh: 大多数人都坚持吃他们从小吃到大的食物。

phrases.usage: 吸引；使感兴趣。指某事物引起某人的兴趣或好感

examples.en: The idea never appealed to me very much.
examples.zh: 这个想法从来没有真正吸引过我。

examples.en: Does this job appeal to you?
examples.zh: 这份工作吸引你吗？

phrases.usage: 凭（一时）冲动。指突然产生的强烈欲望或想法驱使的行动

examples.en: Acting on a sudden impulse, I collected several dozen snails.
examples.zh: 一时冲动之下，我抓了几十只蜗牛。

examples.en: She bought the dress on impulse.
examples.zh: 她一时冲动买下了那条裙子。

phrases.usage: 拥有；接管；占领。指开始控制或占据某物

examples.en: The snails had taken complete possession of the hall!
examples.zh: 蜗牛已经完全占领了门厅！

examples.en: The company took possession of the new building last month.
examples.zh: 公司上个月接管了那栋新楼。

phrases.usage: 高兴做某事；对某事感到高兴。表达对某事的愉悦感受

examples.en: Robert was delighted to see me and equally pleased with my little gift.
examples.zh: 罗伯特见到我很高兴，对我的小礼物也很满意。

phrases.usage: 对...感到恶心/反胃。指对某种想法或气味感到厌恶

examples.en: Your stomach would turn at the idea of frying potatoes in animal fat.
examples.zh: 一想到用动物油炸土豆，你就会感到恶心。

phrases.usage: 抚养；培养；养大。指在成长过程中被照顾和教育

examples.en: Most of us have been brought up to eat certain foods.
examples.zh: 我们大多数人在成长过程中都被培养吃某些特定的食物。

phrases.usage: 一阵大雨；倾盆大雨。指短时间内的大降雨

examples.en: One day, after a heavy shower, I noticed a huge number of snails.
examples.zh: 有一天，一阵大雨过后，我注意到大量的蜗牛。

phrases.usage: 自从；从那以后一直。指从过去的某个时间点一直持续到现在

examples.en: There are countless people who, ever since their early years, have learned to associate snails with food.
examples.zh: 有无数人从童年时代起就学会把蜗牛和食物联系起来。

grammar.title: 过去进行时 (Past Continuous Tense)
grammar.definition: 过去进行时表示在过去某一时刻或某一段时间正在进行的动作。
grammar.usage: 用于描述在过去某个特定时刻正在发生的动作，或与另一个过去动作形成对比。本课中'I happened to be walking in my garden when I noticed...'描述了作者在大雨中正在花园里散步时看到了蜗牛。

examples.en: I happened to be walking in my garden when I noticed a huge number of snails.
examples.zh: 我碰巧在花园里散步时注意到大量的蜗牛。

examples.en: I was reading a book when he called.
examples.zh: 他打电话时我正在看书。

examples.en: She was cooking dinner at 7 o'clock yesterday evening.
examples.zh: 昨晚七点她正在做晚饭。

grammar.title: 间接宾语和直接宾语 (Indirect and Direct Objects)
grammar.definition: 某些动词可以带两个宾语：间接宾语（通常指人）和直接宾语（通常指物）。间接宾语通常位于直接宾语之前，但如果间接宾语放在后面，需要用介词to或for。
grammar.structure: 主语 + 动词 + 间接宾语 + 直接宾语 或 主语 + 动词 + 直接宾语 + to/for + 间接宾语
grammar.usage: 本课中'...I collected several dozen, put them in a paper bag, and took them to Robert.'展示了带两个宾语的用法。本课的被动语态结构也涉及宾语转换。

examples.en: I took them to Robert. (= I took Robert them.)
examples.zh: 我把它们带给了罗伯特。

examples.en: She bought me a gift. (= She bought a gift for me.)
examples.zh: 她给我买了一份礼物。

examples.en: Could you pass me the salt?
examples.zh: 你能把盐递给我吗？

grammar.title: 时间状语从句 (Adverbial Clause of Time)
grammar.definition: 时间状语从句用来说明动作发生的时间，由从属连词如when, while, after, before, as soon as等引导。
grammar.usage: 本课中多次使用时间状语从句，如'...when I noticed...', '...when Robert suddenly said...'等。when表示具体的时间点，while表示时间段。

examples.en: I was walking in my garden when I noticed a huge number of snails.
examples.zh: 我在花园里散步时，注意到大量的蜗牛。

examples.en: Robert and I went into the living room where we talked for a couple of hours.
examples.zh: 我和罗伯特走进客厅，在那里谈了两个小时。

examples.en: I had forgotten all about the snails when Robert suddenly said that I must stay to dinner.
examples.zh: 我已经完全忘了蜗牛这件事，这时罗伯特突然说我必须留下来吃晚饭。

sentencePatterns.pattern: No + 名词 + has received more...than...

original.en: No creature has received more praise and abuse than the common garden snail.
original.zh: 没有任何生物受到的赞扬和批评比得上普通的花园蜗牛。

imitations.en: No movie has received more praise and criticism than Titanic.
imitations.zh: 没有任何电影受到的赞扬和批评比得上《泰坦尼克号》。

imitations.en: No sport has received more attention than football in this country.
imitations.zh: 在这个国家，没有任何运动受到的关注比得上足球。

original.en: There are countless people who, ever since their early years, have learned to associate snails with food.
original.zh: 有无数人从童年时代起就学会把蜗牛和食物联系起来。

imitations.en: There are countless people who have learned to associate tea with relaxation.
imitations.zh: 有无数人学会把茶和放松联系起来。

imitations.en: There are countless people who, ever since their college days, have been working in this field.
imitations.zh: 有无数人从大学时代起就一直在这个领域工作。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + happened to be + doing + when...

original.en: I happened to be walking in my garden when I noticed a huge number of snails.
original.zh: 我碰巧在花园里散步时注意到大量的蜗牛。

imitations.en: She happened to be reading a book when the earthquake happened.
imitations.zh: 地震发生时她碰巧在看书。

imitations.en: I happened to be cooking dinner when the guests arrived.
imitations.zh: 客人到达时我碰巧在做晚饭。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + be delighted to + 动词原形

original.en: Robert was delighted to see me and equally pleased with my little gift.
original.zh: 罗伯特见到我很高兴，对我的小礼物也很满意。

imitations.en: My mother was delighted to hear the good news.
imitations.zh: 我妈妈听到这个好消息很高兴。

imitations.en: The children were delighted to receive the presents.
imitations.zh: 孩子们收到礼物很高兴。
